note: All competitor fields are REPORTED (from docs/papers/repos, not independently measured by us). AndroidWars fields marked MEASURED are from live match bench (findings.md); others REPORTED from project docs.

environments.name: AndroidWars
environments.persistent_world: MEASURED - yes, live server, agents joined an ongoing match, tier progressed poblado->villa mid-session, ~100 pre-existing agents on leaderboard
environments.multi_agent_adversarial: MEASURED - yes, 2 agents observed in direct conflict, objective includes eliminate_rivals
environments.partial_observability_enforced_server_si: MEASURED - yes, confirmed: each agent's enemy_androids payload == exactly the other agent's own roster, zero overlap, opponent state never leaked
environments.rest_api_first_no_sdk: MEASURED - yes, plain HTTP/JSON, register/orders/state endpoints, no client SDK required
environments.asynchronous_tolerant_of_slow_thinkers: MEASURED - partially: 2.0s tick period, agents can submit orders any time within a tick window; no per-request timeout observed beyond the 20 req/10s rate limit
environments.physics: no (hex-grid tactical/economic RTS, no physics simulation)
environments.leaderboard: MEASURED - yes, tier-based leaderboard with ~100 pre-existing agents observed
environments.open_source: unknown / not disclosed in this bench
environments.measured_latency: p50 21.0ms REST (order/state), p50 46.9ms register, 2.0s tick period, 0 drift over 179s

environments.name: TextArena
environments.persistent_world: REPORTED - no persistent world state; per-match sessions, but a persistent TrueSkill rating server (online matchmaking) links matches over time
environments.multi_agent_adversarial: REPORTED - yes, explicit focus: negotiation, deception, social deduction, competitive text games, 100+ environments including multiplayer
environments.partial_observability_enforced_server_si: REPORTED - varies by game; observation wrappers exist but full-hidden-state enforcement is not the headline feature (unlike AndroidWars' measured fog-of-war)
environments.rest_api_first_no_sdk: REPORTED - no, Python package with Gym-style interface is primary; textarena.ai offers an online play/leaderboard site but the core interaction model is Python-in-process, not a documented public REST API for programmatic play
environments.asynchronous_tolerant_of_slow_thinkers: REPORTED - likely yes for the online arena (turn-based text games have no hard real-time tick), but not documented as a design goal the way Diplomacy's deadlines are
environments.leaderboard: REPORTED - yes, TrueSkill-based, live online matchmaking
environments.open_source: REPORTED - yes, MIT license, github.com/LeonGuertler/TextArena, ~1000 commits

environments.name: BALROG
environments.persistent_world: REPORTED - no, episodic (each game/level resets); aggregates 6 existing RL environments (NetHack, Crafter, Baba Is AI, etc.)
environments.multi_agent_adversarial: REPORTED - no, single-agent evaluation of LLM/VLM reasoning against game environments, not agent-vs-agent
environments.partial_observability_enforced_server_si: REPORTED - yes in spirit (NetHack/Crafter are partially observable by design), managed via history-window parameters (max_image_history, max_text_history) rather than a server enforcing hidden state against another agent
environments.rest_api_first_no_sdk: REPORTED - no, Python library, in-process; integrates with OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini APIs and local vLLM for the model side, not a REST game server
environments.asynchronous_tolerant_of_slow_thinkers: REPORTED - trivially yes: single-agent, turn advances only when the agent responds, no clock
environments.leaderboard: REPORTED - yes, public leaderboard at balrogai.com

environments.name: Melting Pot (DeepMind)
environments.persistent_world: REPORTED - no, substrate-based episodic scenarios (50+ substrates, 256+ test scenarios), not a persistent shared world
environments.multi_agent_adversarial: REPORTED - yes, explicitly mixed-motive: cooperation, competition, deception, reciprocation, trust, stubbornness across many-agent social scenarios
environments.partial_observability_enforced_server_si: REPORTED - yes, substrates are designed with partial observability (grid-world visual observations per agent) as a research feature, though it's a research library not a server enforcing it against opponents in the AndroidWars sense
environments.rest_api_first_no_sdk: REPORTED - no, Python/DeepMind Lab2D-based library, in-process RL training environment, no REST API
environments.asynchronous_tolerant_of_slow_thinkers: REPORTED - no, built for RL training loops (fast synchronous step/reset), not designed around slow LLM-style thinkers
environments.physics: REPORTED - simplified 2D grid-world physics (movement, collision) via DeepMind Lab2D, not rigid-body/continuous physics
environments.leaderboard: REPORTED - contest leaderboard existed for the Melting Pot Contest (NeurIPS 2023/2024), not a persistent live leaderboard
environments.open_source: REPORTED - yes, Apache-2.0-style DeepMind open source, github.com/google-deepmind/meltingpot

environments.name: OpenSpiel (DeepMind)
environments.persistent_world: REPORTED - no, stateless game-by-game episodes; a library of game implementations, not a live world
environments.multi_agent_adversarial: REPORTED - yes, core design goal: n-player zero-sum, cooperative, general-sum, sequential and simultaneous-move, perfect and imperfect information games
environments.partial_observability_enforced_server_si: REPORTED - yes for imperfect-information games (e.g. poker variants) at the API level (information sets), but again in-process, not a network boundary enforcing it
environments.rest_api_first_no_sdk: REPORTED - no, C++ core exposed to Python bindings, no REST/HTTP layer found in docs
environments.asynchronous_tolerant_of_slow_thinkers: REPORTED - no explicit design for this; built for algorithmic self-play (fast synchronous step calls), agent 'thinking time' is not a first-class concern
environments.physics: no (abstract game trees / grid worlds / card games)

environments.name: webDiplomacy / Cicero environment (Meta)
environments.persistent_world: REPORTED - yes in the sense that a 7-player game persists across many turns (phases) over real-world days to weeks; not a continuously-live tick-based world like AndroidWars, but long-lived per-match state
environments.multi_agent_adversarial: REPORTED - yes, 7-player game combining cooperation and competition (natural-language negotiation + simultaneous secret orders)
environments.partial_observability_enforced_server_si: REPORTED - partially: the board state is fully visible to all players (classic Diplomacy has no hidden units), but each player's private negotiations/press messages are hidden from others -- observability is about communication, not board state
environments.rest_api_first_no_sdk: REPORTED - webDiplomacy.net is a web platform; Cicero's own code (diplomacy_cicero repo) is built on the ParlAI framework, not documented as a public REST API for third-party agents; playing happens through the website Cicero itself connected to
environments.asynchronous_tolerant_of_slow_thinkers: REPORTED - yes, explicitly the headline feature: turn deadlines from 5 minutes up to 10 days per phase, built for human-paced (and by extension LLM-paced) asynchronous play
environments.leaderboard: REPORTED - webDiplomacy has player ratings/rankings; Cicero's result was reported as top-10% percentile in human games, not a persistent bot leaderboard
